overview: 5c Scapeshift is a Pioneer ramp archetype that uses Scapeshift to rapidly accelerate mana and play oversized threats. By leveraging five-color synergy, the deck efficiently tutors for and casts powerful spells like Lumra, Bellow of the Woods. Formidable Speaker provides additional value by casting cheap spells for free, enabling a smooth transition from early disruption to late-game dominance.
win_conditions: Casting Lumra, Bellow of the Woods for zero mana after ramping
Using Formidable Speaker to repeatedly cast cheap threats and interaction
Playing massive mana-accelerated creatures that outpace opponent defenses

keep_criteria: Hands containing Scapeshift and at least two basic or dual lands
Hands with Formidable Speaker and cheap spells to stabilize
Hands with multiple land ramp sources and a clear win condition
Keep hands with Scapeshift plus one to two lands, or two+ lands paired with cantrips/searchers (e.g., Life from the Loam, Farseek, Kodama's Reach, or fetches).
Against aggro, keep early interaction or discard.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking Scapeshift or any mana acceleration
Hands with four or more lands and no cheap spells
Hands with only high-cost threats and no way to cast them early
Mulligan hands with only one land, no searchers, or no Scapeshift unless heavily supported by cantrips.

early_game: Play lands and hold Scapeshift until you have a safe window to dump lands
Use Formidable Speaker to cast cheap interaction or draw cards
Avoid playing unnecessary lands before Scapeshift to maximize land count for the spell
Turn 1: Play a land and cast a cantrip or early threat if available.
Turn 2: Search for Scapeshift or additional lands using fetches, ramp spells, or search creatures.
Sequence lands to ensure you can reach seven by turn 3-4, prioritizing color fixers and mana sources that support your chosen finisher.
mid_game: Cast Scapeshift to ramp into seven or more lands, drawing into threats
Play Lumra, Bellow of the Woods immediately after ramping to apply pressure
Use Formidable Speaker to cast additional cheap threats or removal from hand
Conserve mana to assemble seven lands without overcommitting.
Use cantrips to dig for Scapeshift or the final land.
Protect your graveyard if running Life from the Loam, and avoid unnecessary land drops that delay your win condition.
Commit threats only when you have a clear path to seven lands or can protect your combo.
Hold back if the opponent has hard interaction
use cantrips to dig around it rather than overextending with vulnerable permanents.
late_game: Maintain board presence with oversized creatures that are difficult to remove
Cycle through cheap spells using Formidable Speaker to outvalue opponents
Protect your mana base and win condition with available interaction
Scapeshift into seven Forests for Tron (Lumra/Nissa), seven Islands for Storm (Formidable Speaker/Emrakul), or seven Mountains for burn.
Tron wins via stack-independent triggers, while Storm wins quickly on the stack.
Go all-in when you have Scapeshift in hand, six lands on the battlefield, and cantrips to find the seventh.
Commit when the opponent has no interaction left or when you can safely resolve your finisher.

key_cards.name: Scapeshift
key_cards.notes: Discard lands to put more onto the battlefield, enabling zero-cost casting of Lumra and drawing into threats. Hold until you have enough lands to dump safely.

key_cards.name: Lumra, Bellow of the Woods
key_cards.role: Primary win condition and board presence
key_cards.notes: Cast for significantly reduced mana after Scapeshift. Use its trample ability to push damage quickly while surviving combat.

key_cards.name: Formidable Speaker
key_cards.role: Value generator and cheap spell enabler
key_cards.notes: Enter the battlefield to cast spells with mana value 3 or less for free. Use it to tutor, stabilize, or cast additional threats without spending mana.

key_cards.name: Five-Color Land Base
key_cards.notes: Access to all five colors ensures optimal land choices and spell selection, allowing Scapeshift to reliably find the right lands and threats without color screw.

sequencing_tips: Play lands before casting Scapeshift to maximize the number of lands you can put onto the battlefield
Hold Scapeshift until you have a clear path to play Lumra or another high-cost threat
Use Formidable Speaker's triggered ability before or after Scapeshift depending on whether you need immediate interaction or want to save cheap spells for later
Avoid overcommitting to the board early if you plan to Scapeshift, as discarded lands can be replaced
Scapeshift is a sorcery, so it's vulnerable to instant-speed interaction
always have a backup plan or protection.
The five-color mana base is complex, so practice land sequencing to avoid mana screw.
Tron is your most reliable finisher against control, while Storm offers speed but requires stack protection.
Avoid overcommitting to one finisher type
keep your options open based on the board state.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early interaction and hold Scapeshift until you can play a large blocker or win condition. Use Formidable Speaker to cast cheap removal or draw cards to stabilize.
watch_for: Board wipes that target low-toughness creatures

matchups.advice: Ramp quickly to bypass early disruption and play unblockable threats. Use Formidable Speaker to cast interaction for free and protect your win conditions.
watch_for: Hand disruption targeting your cheap spells

matchups.advice: Race with Lumra and use Formidable Speaker to generate more value than their incremental advantage plays. Time your Scapeshift to avoid their removal spells.
watch_for: Tempo plays that disrupt your ramp sequence

matchups.advice: Use Tron to bypass the stack, or split your combo across multiple targets. Use discard to remove counterspells, or win via instant-speed cantrips and landfall triggers that don't rely on Scapeshift resolving.
Race them with faster finishes (Storm or Tron), use counterspells/discard to disrupt their mana, or win via burn if they overextend. Prioritize speed and interaction.

Grind for Scapeshift and early lands. Use graveyard hate to disrupt their Life from the Loam or landfall triggers. Sideboard in counterspells and discard to disrupt their combo, and prioritize protecting your own finisher.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, discard spells, and land destruction.
Note if the opponent is playing sweepers or targeted removal aimed at your lands or Scapeshift.
Track their graveyard if they run Life from the Loam or similar hate.
